changes.change: NO schema change. The redesigned invite tool (S14) needs human-readable named slug codes (e.g. 'neon-toaster-mongoose'), multi-use with a uses-remaining count, an optional note, and a captain pre-approve flag — every one of these is ALREADY a column: code (free-text PK, any slug works), note (text), max_uses (int) + use_count (int, atomic consume via consumeInviteCode), requires_approval (bool). findInviteCodeByCode already powers the board's availability hint. The redesign is satisfied entirely at the app layer (slug-format validation '3-48 chars, lowercase letters/digits/hyphens' per the board's 'invalid' state, friendly-name generation, the shuffle button).
changes.reason: The brief flags invite codes 'becoming' human-readable multi-use named slugs with note + pre-approve as the headline redesign, but the live schema already models all of it. The only board feature with NO column is per-redemption rank stamping — and even that exists (assigned_rank). Nothing to migrate.
changes.boardEvidence: 23-s14-invite-tool.txt: NoteField textarea, CaptainOptions block with a 'Pre-approve whoever signs up' checkbox + 'How many people can use this code' stepper (value 1), CodeInput shows 'neon-toaster-mongoose' in JetBrains Mono with a shuffle button, AvailHint 'is available.'/'already taken'/'3–48 chars, lowercase letters / digits / hyphens.' SuccessCard meta 'Can be used by 1 person.' + 'They'll be pre-approved — no captain sign-off needed.' 12-s03-invite-gate.txt placeholder 'CAMP-XXXX-XXXX' is the OLD format; the redesign moves to word-slugs but the column is format-agnostic.

changes.change: OPTIONAL/app-layer only: the gate board (S03) still shows the legacy 'CAMP-XXXX-XXXX' placeholder while the tool board (S14) mints word-slugs. No column change — both are valid text PKs. Reconcile the gate placeholder copy to the slug format. Flag only because the two boards diverge on the displayed format, not the storage.
changes.reason: Detecting iteration drift is part of the pass: the invite-gate board predates the slug redesign. This is a copy reconciliation, not a data-model change.
changes.boardEvidence: 12-s03-invite-gate.txt InputField overrides ['Invite code','CAMP-XXXX-XXXX'] and sample 'CAMP-7F3A-K92B' vs 23-s14-invite-tool.txt code 'neon-toaster-mongoose'.

changes.change: NO schema change. All 11 OB-step boards (39–49) map 1:1 onto the EXISTING burner_profile catalogue in apps/web/lib/questionnaire.ts (version 2026.05.29-v8): page ids profile_photo, about_you, bio, burn_ideas, team_interests_intro (intro), team_interests, cooking_competency, hardware_competency, leadership_logistics, burn_history, burn_intent, dietary — and every question id (profile.image, birthday, phone, country, id.type, id.number, bio.statement, ideas.this_year, team_interest.*, competency.cooking/hardware, team_lead.interests, logistics.driving/onsite_before/onsite_after, history.camp404_years/afrikaburn_count/other_burns, intent.this_year, dietary.dislikes/allergies/notes) plus option text matches verbatim. Answers persist as a flat jsonb map on burner_profiles.responses. The onboarding question set does NOT diverge from the questionnaire catalogue.
changes.reason: The brief asks whether OB Step 01–11 match the questionnaire catalogue — they match exactly. The redesign is a per-step page LAYOUT change (one question-group per full-height frame) with no new question data, so the data substrate is untouched.
changes.boardEvidence: Boards 39–49 prompts/options match questionnaire.ts lines 60–390 line-for-line, e.g. 45-ob-step-07 'Good cook — I can create recipes'/'I might burn recipes' == competency.cooking steps; 46-ob-step-08 team-lead checkboxes == team_lead.interests multi_select over TEAMS; 48-ob-step-10 six intent labels == intent.this_year scale steps; 49-ob-step-11 12 DIETARY_INGREDIENTS == dietary.dislikes/allergies.

changes.change: NO schema change (jsonb stores a number regardless). NOTE the only OB-step divergence: OB Step 06 renders the team-interest control as a 7-segment 0–6 scale, but the catalogue defines team_interest.* as a slider min:0 max:5 step:1 (range 0–5). Reconcile the board to 0–5 OR widen the catalogue's max to 6 — a code/config change in questionnaire.ts, not a Postgres migration.
changes.reason: validateResponses enforces slider value in [min,max]=[0,5]; a stored 6 would be rejected. This is a catalogue-vs-board mismatch to resolve before build, but it is a code constant, not a schema column.
changes.boardEvidence: 44-ob-step-06-team-interests.txt draws 'Seg 0'…'Seg 6' (7 segments, selected Seg 5/Seg 3) vs questionnaire.ts:184-186 min:0 max:5 step:1.

changes.change: NO schema change for the data. The roster status pills 'Pending / Accepted / Rejected' map onto the existing approval_status enum [pending, approved, rejected] — 'Accepted' is a display relabel of 'approved'. The roster filter chips (All/Pending/Captains) and stat tiles (MEMBERS/APPROVED/INCOMPLETE) are all derived counts over approval_status + rank, not new columns. No roster status FIELD is required.
changes.reason: The brief asks specifically about roster status fields. The board's three states are exactly the three enum members; 'Accepted' vs 'approved' is a label-only divergence handled in code. No migration.
changes.boardEvidence: 26-s17-captain-mgmt.txt Status pills 'Pending' (#e0a800), 'Accepted' (#2fbf71), 'Rejected' (#f83e5a); chips 'All 42 / Pending 3 / Captains 4'. 38-s17-roster-mobile.txt stat tiles MEMBERS 42 / APPROVED 39 / INCOMPLETE 7 (INCOMPLETE = derived from pending required_actions, not a stored field).

changes.change: ADD a new table to back the double-opt-in 'Assign captain rank' flow shown on all three S17 roster boards. Minimum shape: id uuid PK, target_user_id uuid FK→users, requested_by_user_id uuid FK→users, status enum ['sent','accepted','declined','cancelled'] default 'sent', created_at, decided_at. The existing setUserRank is a one-sided direct write and CANNOT model a two-sided handshake (captain sends → target accepts in their OWN app before rank flips).
changes.reason: This is the single genuinely new persisted concept in the redesign with NO backing table. The contract has no captain-promotion flow at all. Rank can only flip to 'captain' once the target accepts, so the pending request must be durable state, not a transient action.
changes.boardEvidence: 26-s17-captain-mgmt.txt DialogScrim 'Make Nova Reyes a captain?' with a two-step tracker ('You send the request' Done / 'They accept in their app' Pending) and copy 'two-sided agreement, so you can't assign it for them'. Same flow on 37-terminal-console and 38-mobile roster iterations.

changes.change: LIKELY NO schema change. The S27 'required queue' (multiple named questionnaires: Safety, Dietary, Agreements, one shown 'locked' at op:0.55, '1 more required before you're unlocked') is already expressible via the generic required_actions engine (one pending+blocking row per obligation, getPendingRequiredActions returns them oldest-first as the gate order) fed by questionnaire_activations. The member-facing 'queue list' and sequential 'Start next questionnaire' are a NEW read/UX over existing rows. The only soft gap is a 'locked / not-yet-available' state for a queue item — required_action_status has no 'locked' member; today an item is simply pending or absent. If sequential UNLOCK (item N hidden until N-1 done) is required, that ordering/lock is best modelled in app logic, not a new enum value.
changes.reason: The brief flags whether roster/queue introduces status fields. The queue is satisfied by existing required_actions; only the 'locked' visual MIGHT imply a new status, but it is more naturally a derived/app concern than a column. Flagging as a watch-item, not a required migration.
changes.boardEvidence: 36-s27-questionnaire-complete-queue.txt 'Required Queue List' three Card rows (Safety, Dietary, Agreements) with the third at op:0.55 (locked), 'Variant More Required' shows '1 more required before you're unlocked' + 'Start next questionnaire'.

changes.change: OPTIONAL, app-layer or minor add. The roster boards render an '@handle' per member (@novareyes, @nova). The existing telegram_handle column can serve this, OR it is a derived slug of display_name. Only add a dedicated users.handle text column if product wants a first-class, editable, unique username distinct from the Telegram handle. Not depicted as editable anywhere (no profile-edit handle field), so most likely reuse telegram_handle / derive — no migration.
changes.reason: Handle appears only as read-only roster decoration with no edit surface, so it does not clearly demand a new column. Flagged so the choice (reuse telegram_handle vs new field) is made deliberately.
changes.boardEvidence: 26-s17-captain-mgmt.txt Handle column '@novareyes'/'@juleso' etc.; 38-s17-roster-mobile.txt '@nova'/'@jules'. No handle field on 18-s09-profile-view.txt or the profile-edit board.

changes.change: OPTIONAL, app-layer. The primitive kit surfaces 'Invites expire after 7 days'. The expires_at column already exists; a 7-day default is set at creation time in createInviteCode, not a schema default. No migration; just decide whether the invite tool stamps expires_at = now + 7d on mint.
changes.reason: A business rule surfaced on a board (S24) that the schema already accommodates via an existing nullable column. Listed for completeness; not a structural change.
changes.boardEvidence: 33-s24-primitive-kit alert copy 'Invites expire after 7 days' (per board census). invite_codes.expires_at column exists (schema.ts:322).

nukeRationale: A clean DB reset is NOT warranted — the delta is small and almost entirely additive. The two headline redesign intents the brief worried about (invite codes → human-readable, multi-use, named slugs with note + pre-approve; the OB Step 01–11 onboarding question set) require ZERO schema changes: the live invite_codes table already has code (free-slug PK), note, max_uses, use_count (atomic consume), requires_approval, and assigned_rank; and the 11 onboarding step boards map 1:1 onto the existing burner_profile questionnaire catalogue (v8) persisting to burner_profiles.responses jsonb. Roster status pills are just the existing approval_status enum with one label rename (approved→'Accepted'). The ONLY genuinely new persisted concept is one additive table+enum for the captain-promotion double-opt-in (captain_promotion_requests). Everything else is either already-present columns, derived counts, app-layer validation/copy, or new read-models over existing rows. One additive table plus a couple of optional/deferred columns is a trivial forward migration with no destructive column drops or enum-member removals — there is nothing to gain from nuking, and a reset would needlessly discard god accounts, lineage (users.invite_code), and audit history that the schema is explicitly designed to preserve across the yearly reset.
notes: Headline: the redesign is far less schema-disruptive than the brief implies. Confirmed against live code, not just docs: (1) packages/db/src/invite-codes.ts already implements multi-use named-slug invites with note + requires_approval + atomic consume + an availability-check (findInviteCodeByCode) that exactly powers the board's available/taken hint — the invite redesign is 100% app-layer. (2) apps/web/lib/questionnaire.ts (version 2026.05.29-v8) already defines every page/question/option the OB Step boards show; the boards are a per-step LAYOUT redesign of an unchanged question set. The 11-vs-12 step-counter difference is a display artifact (the intro page isn't counted), NOT a data change. (3) The single real schema gap is the captain-promotion double-opt-in (S17 roster boards) which needs a new captain_promotion_requests table + status enum because setUserRank is a one-sided write that can't model a two-sided handshake. Watch-items (not migrations): team_interest scale 0–6 on the board vs 0–5 in the catalogue (fix one constant), an S27 'locked' queue-item state (better as app logic over required_actions than a new enum member), the roster @handle (reuse telegram_handle or derive), and a 7-day invite expiry default (set at mint via existing expires_at). Stale schema note worth fixing while in there: schema.ts:454 still claims a team-lead 'should carry rank=team_lead', but team_lead is not a rank enum member (derived only) — the roster boards correctly show 'team lead' as a derived pill, reinforcing that the stored rank stays {captain, member}. PII flag: roster boards show member email in plaintext in the detail panel (26-s17-captain-mgmt.txt 'nova.reyes@gmail.com'); email is not a screened/encrypted field, so this is a presentation/privacy decision rather than a schema one, but worth raising with the data owner.
